nodes.label: The Lo-Fi Signal
nodes.description: Lo-fi indie rock woven with electronic textures -- loops, samples, synthesizer washes, drum machine patterns. Music as transmission: warm, hazy, carrying melody through static. Neither indie nor electronic dominates. Both handmade. Both lo-fi. E=4.

nodes.label: Ahead of Its Time -- Indie/Electronic Before the Postal Service
nodes.description: Salt Sinks and Sugar Floats (1997) predates the Postal Service's Give Up (2003) by six years. 764-HERO was experimenting with the indie/electronic crossover before it had a name. The pioneers dissolved circa 2003, just as the genre they anticipated exploded.

nodes.label: Up Records -- New Label in the Engine
nodes.description: Bellingham/Seattle indie label founded by Chris Takino in 1994. Home to Modest Mouse (early), the Microphones, Mirah, 764-HERO. Lo-fi, experimental, DIY. Joins Sub Pop, Kill Rock Stars, K Records, Suicide Squeeze, Barsuk, Sargent House in the engine's label map.

nodes.label: E=6 Corridor Ends at Degree 112
nodes.description: Four consecutive E=6 (108-111) then E=4 at 112. The maximum sustained for four degrees then dropped. The cliff. Maximum to moderate in one degree. The quiet after the storm.

nodes.label: Capitol Hill Streak Continues (110-112)
nodes.description: Three consecutive Capitol Hill acts: Kinski (110, E=6), Helms Alee (111, E=6), 764-HERO (112, E=4). The neighborhood's range: from maximum-energy heavy music to contemplative lo-fi. Capitol Hill is diverse enough to sustain both extremes.

nodes.label: Modest Mouse Connection -- Up Records Ecosystem
nodes.description: 764-HERO and Modest Mouse were both core Up Records artists. Isaac Brock and John Atkins were contemporaries in the same small-label ecosystem. Two expressions of the same impulse: Modest Mouse toward angular indie rock, 764-HERO toward electronic-infused contemplation.

cross_references.relationship: E=6 TO E=4 DROP. Consecutive degrees. Helms Alee at E=6 maximum. 764-HERO at E=4. The E=6 corridor ends. Both Capitol Hill.
ELECTRONIC EXPERIMENTATION. Both incorporate electronic elements into guitar-based music. Both E=4. Different approaches to the indie/electronic intersection.
ELECTRONIC PRODUCTION. Both use electronic production as fundamental tool. Chong is beat-driven hip-hop (E=5). 764-HERO is lo-fi indie (E=4).
CAPITOL HILL CONTEMPORARIES. Ben Gibbard later formed the Postal Service -- the crossover 764-HERO anticipated.
CAPITOL HILL STREAK. Three consecutive (110-112). E=6 to E=6 to E=4. Capitol Hill's range from loudest to quietest.
